stem: RAM and ROM are both types of primary storage inside a computer.
model_answer: RAM is volatile: its contents are lost when power is removed. It is read/write and holds the programs and data currently being used by the CPU, so it must be fast. ROM is non-volatile: its contents persist when power is off. It is read-only (from the computer's point of view) and typically stores the boot firmware (BIOS/UEFI) needed to start the system.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare RAM and ROM. Refer to volatility, read/write access, and typical purpose.

mark_points.text: RAM is volatile; ROM is non-volatile
accepted_alternatives: RAM loses data when powered off, ROM keeps data

mark_points.text: RAM can be read from and written to; ROM is (effectively) read-only
accepted_alternatives: RAM is read/write, ROM is read-only

mark_points.text: RAM holds programs and data currently in use by the CPU
accepted_alternatives: RAM stores running programs and working data

mark_points.text: ROM stores firmware such as the BIOS/UEFI used to start the computer
